author: @MichaelAckerman
text:
There is a video of a single tooth helical involute gear you might like. I understand that involute gears have advantages over cycloidal. Note that the end face is heart ❤ shaped rather than circular. YouTube suppresses links, so search for:
 1枚歯インボリュートはすば歯車 (試作)

author: @stuffoflardohfortheloveof
text: Brill Rob! And another thumbs up for tinkercad (again) 👍

author: @BogdanRublenko
text: Hello, I tried to replicate this experiment with tea and than with tannin both several times and I don't get the waterproofing effect I used bio glicerine (glycerol) because as a person you cannot buy glicerine any more the instructions followed to the teath. And also tried to do the hemp seaweed plastic with a hemp cloth and it just made the material a little hard but very easy to bind, seaweed used was one kelp and the other time spiruline.Can you help me understand why this can happen?

author: @bigchrisrogers
text: One of the things I love about tinkercad is that it makes you think about how to construct your model.  Rather than just select the software feature from a dropdown menu, working with the relatively primitive capabilities seems to take me back to the drawing board.  As a young engineer in the early eighties I spent a lot of time with pencil, ruler, pair of compasses and paper and understanding how forms were created in order to draw them for the toolroom.  Robert, it seems to me that you enjoy such similar challenges with Tinkercad?

author: @growapairepaire7354
text:
Hi Robert, after reading your channels description I would like to ask you if you have already covered 'free' energy? I watched and ex aeronautical engineer put magnets and zinc plates together and he managed to get voltage. I followed the same and got nothing!  I have seen magnets used in enhancing plant growth and seed germination, as well as inducing electrical current and flow as in a starter motor or alternator. So what could I be doing wrong? 
Thanking you in advance.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you probably didn't do anything wrong mate - most of the free energy stuff is just made for views and doesn't work

author: @EricFranklin-d2v
text: I made some of these about six months ago with a Blender tutorial. Fumbled around for hours, and finally got it. Not exactly intuative.  And, it wasnt parametric, so to make a change I'd have to do it all over again, every time. Yours is by far a much better tutorial. Both this one and the code block parametric video. Thank you! I feel empowered now😁

author: @grendel1960a
text: lovely, in a parametric system you can do that with a single commend, just tell it the rotation of the original shape and the axis while extruding, but good to see you can do it in tinkercad too.
